title: Should I wait for a re dub
display_title: Should I wait for a re dub
wikitext:
[]: Should I wait for a re-dub?             Quote Post by SHINOBI-03 » Sun Apr 10, 2011 7:42 am For now, I've watched the series via fansubs. I don't own an official release of the series. I want to buy the Platinum version, but at the same time, I don't want to. Why? because I'm waiting for any possible news of a re-release with a better re-dub from FUNimation who owns the dub rights now. Sure, some would say I should buy what existed for years and engraved in the fandom, but I have a problem with some of the dub voices. Especially whoever voiced Ritsuku because she was goddamn awful from what I've seen. So, what you think? Should I buy the ADV dub version? Or wait for a FUNimation re-dub that may or may not happen?

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sun Apr 10, 2011 7:45 am I don´t think that they´ll re-dub the original series. :/ The english dub of Rebuild is probably the closest you can get, probably.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 10, 2011 10:41 am Just buy Platinum so that you actually own the original, kick-ass series, and ignore the English dub track to the best of your ability.

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Sun Apr 10, 2011 10:57 am >waiting for a good dub of NGE >might as well say you're never intending to buy it

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Apr 10, 2011 11:02 am Reichu wrote: Just buy Platinum so that you actually own the original, kick-ass series, and ignore the English dub track to the best of your ability.

[]:             Quote Post by SHINOBI-03 » Sun Apr 10, 2011 12:03 pm Reichu wrote: Just buy Platinum so that you actually own the original, kick-ass series, and ignore the English dub track to the best of your ability.

[]:             Quote Post by LordEvan88 » Sun Apr 10, 2011 12:05 pm As someone who does not outright hate the dub, but who does acknowledge that it really does suck a lot more than I remember (I just restarted the series and its kind of night and day with Rebuild) I feel it does improve over time. However the odds of it ever getting redubbed are slim. Whatever remains of ADV will need to have it pried from their cold dead hands if they want the license to use the series (Not the movies, mind you). End of Evangelion might get it at some point. I am not sure how licensing works, I was under the impression that re-dubbing was obligatory but they kept the old dub of FLCL so that may spell a precedent being set.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Sun Apr 10, 2011 1:23 pm You could just suck it up and buy the Platinum which will most likely be better than whatever FUNimation will churn out. Plus, the chances they're going to re-release the series again within a year or two is very very small. Platinum is clearly the most superior release of the series thus far and likely will remain so.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Apr 10, 2011 1:29 pm When I look at this thread, I really feel as if I were the only person that liked the English dub over the Japanese dub.. is this so? It's probably because I heard the English before the Japanese....

[]:             Quote Post by Seen » Sun Apr 10, 2011 2:38 pm I'm hoping for the same thing. Sadly, I doubt it will happen. I really don't want to waste 60 bucks on something that I already have in questionable-form with higher quality.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Apr 10, 2011 3:06 pm 1-14 subbed 15-26 dubbed movies subbed Problem solved.

[]:             Quote Post by LordEvan88 » Sun Apr 10, 2011 9:17 pm Stryker wrote: When I look at this thread, I really feel as if I were the only person that liked the English dub over the Japanese dub.. is this so? It's probably because I heard the English before the Japanese....

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 11, 2011 12:12 am Stryker wrote: When I look at this thread, I really feel as if I were the only person that liked the English dub over the Japanese dub.. is this so? It's probably because I heard the English before the Japanese....

[]:             Quote Post by Darkman.exe213 » Mon Apr 11, 2011 1:41 am LordEvan88 wrote: However the odds of it ever getting redubbed are slim. Whatever remains of ADV will need to have it pried from their cold dead hands if they want the license to use the series (Not the movies, mind you).

[]:             Quote Post by MasterRah » Mon Apr 11, 2011 11:42 am Stryker wrote: When I look at this thread, I really feel as if I were the only person that liked the English dub over the Japanese dub.. is this so? It's probably because I heard the English before the Japanese....

[]:             Quote Post by zuka » Mon Apr 11, 2011 8:57 pm I like the English dub of the original series a lot more than the Japanese, simply because that's what I grew up with. But having rewatched it, it gets a lot better as the series progresses - Ritsuku is the worst until the second half, where you dont notice it.

[]:             Quote Post by Seen » Mon Apr 11, 2011 9:23 pm Darkman.exe213 wrote: That said, I wouldn't mind seeing a new dub with Brina Palencia as Rei. Not that I have any problem with Amanda Winn Lee being Rei (other than the commentary of doom), but Brina brings just a bit more frailness that works with the character.

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Mon Apr 11, 2011 9:56 pm Stryker wrote: When I look at this thread, I really feel as if I were the only person that liked the English dub over the Japanese dub.. is this so? It's probably because I heard the English before the Japanese....

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Apr 11, 2011 10:05 pm just get the Platinum Collection for now and enjoy it you may get your wish in time for a new English-speaking dub, but IMO that will only happen if some major media conglomerate like Disney, News Corp., Time Warner or Comcast buys the distribution/licensing rights from FUNimation, and for now practically NO ONE in mainstream entertainment in the Western Hemisphere takes anime srsly as a reliably profitable genre, especially older anime

[]:             Quote Post by Seen » Mon Apr 11, 2011 10:36 pm THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: just get the Platinum Collection for now and enjoy it you may get your wish in time for a new English-speaking dub, but IMO that will only happen if some major media conglomerate like Disney, News Corp., Time Warner or Comcast buys the distribution/licensing rights from FUNimation, and for now practically NO ONE in mainstream entertainment in the Western Hemisphere takes anime srsly as a reliably profitable genre, especially older anime
